issue_title: terraform 0.11.13 crash on init when remote backend was previously configured with 0.12
issue_body:
terraform 0.11.13 crashes when you run `terraform init` in a directory that was previously configured with a remote backend & terraform 0.12.

1: config w/ remote backend, init/apply/destroy
2: switch back to terraform 0.11.13 and run `terrafrom init`. [crash.log](https://gist.github.com/mildwonkey/b06aebc461e55bc91d29fe2d4fa350a5)

Expected output: a friendly error message that terraform needs to be upgraded.
pr_title: backend/remote: fix panic when loading a 0.12 cached backend
pr_body:
The remote backend schema changed in terraform 0.12, causing a panic
when 0.11 tries to load it. While we do not support downgrading
configurations, we should prevent panics where possible.

We may consider some form of backend schema versioning in the future.

Fixes #21190

NOTE: The error message is not terribly accurate or informative:

```
Error loading previously configured backend:
Error configuring the backend "remote": the cached backend configuration is not valid for this version of terraform

Please update the configuration in your Terraform files to fix this error.
If you'd like to update the configuration interactively without storing
the values in your configuration, run "terraform init".
```

I could add a specific return error type and add a new message that specifically states that an upgrade is required, though I have doubts about the long-term accuracy of such a message. This is also (I believe) a fairly minor edge case, which is why I did not do so yet.
